senses.gloss: a number of horses assembled from every quarter for a race
damma, ḍamma, the diacritic in the Arabic script that represents the vowel /u/, appearing as a small curl placed above a letter: ـُ

etymology.text: Instance noun of the Form I verb ضَمَّ (ḍamma, “to embrace; to gather; to compress the lips”), from the root ض م م (ḍ m m). When used to describe the vowel ـُ (u), ضَمَّة (ḍamma) means “an instance of [lip-]rounding”, since u is the only short vowel in Arabic that is pronounced with lip-rounding.

body.text: [inf. n. un. of 1 (q. v.): and as such signifying]
A number of horses assembled from every quarter for a race:
(K, TA:) thus called because so assembled. (TA.) ―         -b3-  [And, as a conventional term in lexicology and grammar, The
